annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem (groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuotientGroup.quotientKerEquivRange` gives the explicit isomorphism `G / ker φ ≃* range φ` for a group homomorphism `φ`.

annotations.label: Second isomorphism theorem (groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuotientGroup.quotientInfEquivProdNormalQuotient` is the classical second isomorphism `H/(H ∩ N) ≃* HN/N` for `N` normal.

annotations.label: Projective linear group via second isomorphism theorem

anchor.snippet: An application of the second isomorphism theorem identifies projective linear groups

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `PGL(n,R) := GL n R ⧸ Subgroup.center (GL n R)` directly but does not phrase its identification as an application of the second isomorphism theorem.

annotations.label: Third isomorphism theorem and correspondence (groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuotientGroup.quotientQuotientEquivQuotient` provides the canonical isomorphism `(G/N)/(M/N) ≃* G/M` when `N ≤ M`.

annotations.label: Correspondence/lattice theorem (groups)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuotientGroup.comapMk'OrderIso` is the lattice isomorphism between subgroups of `G/N` and subgroups of `G` containing `N`.

annotations.label: Zassenhaus (butterfly) lemma

anchor.snippet: The Zassenhaus lemma (also known as the butterfly lemma) is sometimes called the fourth isomorphism theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib does not yet contain a formal statement of the Zassenhaus/butterfly lemma identifying the canonical isomorphism between the two refinement quotients arising from subgroups H, K of a group with normal subgroups H' ⊴ H, K' ⊴ K.

annotations.label: Splitting lemma

anchor.snippet: left splits and right splits are equivalent by the splitting lemma

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes splittings of short exact sequences via `CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Splitting` in abelian categories; the classical equivalence of left/right splits invoked here is captured through this API rather than as a single named lemma.

annotations.label: Nine lemma

anchor.snippet: The third isomorphism theorem is generalized by the nine lemma to abelian categories

annotations.note: Mathlib does not yet contain a general statement of the 3×3 (nine) lemma for abelian categories.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem (rings)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `RingHom.quotientKerEquivRange` gives `R/ker f ≃+* f.range` for a ring homomorphism `f`.

annotations.label: Second isomorphism theorem (rings)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `RingCon.comapQuotientEquivRange` is labelled as the second isomorphism theorem for rings via ring congruences, generalizing the classical `(S+I)/I ≅ S/(S∩I)` statement.

annotations.label: Third isomorphism theorem and correspondence (rings)

anchor.snippet: Let R be a ring, and I an ideal of R

annotations.note: Mathlib's `DoubleQuot.quotQuotEquivQuotOfLE` is the third isomorphism theorem for rings: `(R/I)/(J/I) ≃+* R/J` when `I ≤ J`.

annotations.label: Correspondence theorem (rings)

anchor.snippet: is an inclusion -preserving bijection between the set of subrings

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Ideal.relIsoOfSurjective` gives the order isomorphism `Ideal S ≃o {p : Ideal R // comap f ⊥ ≤ p}` for a surjective ring homomorphism.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem (modules)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `LinearMap.quotKerEquivRange` provides the canonical `M/ker f ≃ₗ[R] range f` for a linear map `f`.

annotations.label: Second isomorphism theorem (modules)
annotations.note: Mathlib's `LinearMap.quotientInfEquivSupQuotient` is the second isomorphism theorem for modules: `p / (p ⊓ p') ≃ₗ (p ⊔ p') / p'`.

annotations.label: Third isomorphism theorem and correspondence (modules)

anchor.snippet: Let M be a module, T a submodule of M

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Submodule.quotientQuotientEquivQuotient` provides `(M/S)/(T/S) ≃ₗ[R] M/T` when `S ≤ T`.

annotations.label: Correspondence theorem (modules)

anchor.snippet: There is a bijection between the submodules of

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Submodule.comapMkQRelIso` gives the order isomorphism `Submodule R (M ⧸ p) ≃o Set.Ici p` between submodules of `M/p` and submodules of `M` containing `p`.

annotations.label: Rank–nullity theorem (modules section)

anchor.snippet: all of these theorems follow from the rank–nullity theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` states `finrank (M ⧸ p) + finrank p = finrank M` for finite-dimensional vector spaces, the rank–nullity identity invoked here.

annotations.label: Congruence on an algebra
annotations.note: Mathlib has structure-specific congruences (`Con` for magmas/monoids, `AddCon`, `RingCon`) and `Setoid`, but no fully general universal-algebra congruence definition.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem (universal algebra)
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the first isomorphism theorem for `Con`/`RingCon`/`Setoid` separately but lacks a single universal-algebra statement.

annotations.label: Second isomorphism theorem (universal algebra)
annotations.note: Only structure-specific versions (e.g. `Con.comapQuotientEquiv`, `RingCon.comapQuotientEquivRange`, `LinearMap.quotientInfEquivSupQuotient`) exist; no general universal-algebra version is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Third isomorphism theorem (universal algebra)
annotations.note: Mathlib has `Setoid.quotientQuotientEquivQuotient` plus the `Con` and `RingCon` variants, but no statement at the level of universal algebra.

annotations.label: Correspondence theorem (universal algebra)

anchor.snippet: the set of all congruences on

annotations.note: Mathlib has correspondence/lattice theorems for subgroups, ideals, and submodules but no general bijection between congruences containing a given congruence and congruences on the quotient algebra.
